CheckTitle: AI Agent Outbound Network Access Must Be Filtered by Destination
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Network Security**. Verifies that every AI agent has egress filtering in place to restrict outbound network connections to an approved set of destinations. Agents that can reach arbitrary internet endpoints are a data-exfiltration vector if compromised or manipulated via prompt injection.
Risk: An agent with unrestricted egress can be instructed by a prompt injection attack to exfiltrate sensitive data (PII, secrets, embeddings) to an attacker-controlled server. Without egress filtering there is no network-layer control to prevent this.

Code.Other:
1. Set has_egress_filtering: true in the agent manifest.
2. Apply network policies (Kubernetes NetworkPolicy, AWS Security Groups, GCP VPC firewall rules) to allow only required outbound destinations.
3. Route all egress through a forward proxy (e.g., Squid, AWS Network Firewall) with a domain allow-list.

Recommendation.Text: Restrict agent egress to a minimal allow-list of required endpoints using cloud-native network policies and a forward proxy. Deny all outbound traffic by default and alert on policy violations.

Notes: ASPM-040 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
